steps.title: Find the contrast
steps.content: The first sentence describes the poem as “barren terrain for critical analysis.” The second sentence says Whitaker “finds fertile ground” in it. These directly oppose each other — the transition must bridge this contradiction.

steps.title: Choose the concession word
steps.content: Nonetheless works as a concession word: despite the poem's barren appearance, Whitaker finds analytical depth in it. The others express agreement (Likewise), sequence (In turn), or restatement (That is) — not contrast. That is the correct answer.
